machinery_genes.quote: The cluster contains all 13 essential core conserved T6SS components... The 13 core components have all been shown to be essential for secretion in at least one organism and were recently designated TssA to TssM in an attempt to unify the nomenclature.
machinery_genes.note: Named collectively within the 'TssA to TssM' core set documented for the single S. marcescens Db10 T6SS cluster (SMA2243-SMA2281). No individual functional-characterization quote naming TssA alone was found; role from apparatus brief vocabulary. Moderate-confidence call (collective naming).

machinery_genes.quote: The cluster contains all 13 essential core conserved T6SS components... were recently designated TssA to TssM in an attempt to unify the nomenclature.
machinery_genes.note: Part of the collectively-named TssA-TssM core set of the Db10 cluster. TssB = VipA-family inner sheath subunit (apparatus brief). Moderate-confidence (collective naming).

machinery_genes.quote: The cluster contains all 13 essential core conserved T6SS components... were recently designated TssA to TssM in an attempt to unify the nomenclature.
machinery_genes.note: Part of the collectively-named TssA-TssM core set. TssC = VipB-family outer sheath subunit (apparatus brief). Moderate-confidence (collective naming).

machinery_genes.quote: The 16-kDa band visible in the secreted proteins of the wild-type cells... was excised and subjected to in-gel trypsin digestion followed by ESI-MSMS identification... as SMA2263 (Hcp).
machinery_genes.note: Hcp (TssD) directly identified by mass spectrometry as a secreted product of the Db10 T6SS; locus SMA2263. Brief counts Hcp as machinery (secreted tube). The cluster contains one Hcp homologue plus two others elsewhere in the genome.

machinery_genes.quote: we constructed in-frame deletion mutants in genes encoding... TssE (SMA2271)
machinery_genes.note: TssE explicitly named and mutated (SMA2271) as a core component of the Db10 T6SS.

machinery_genes.quote: The cluster contains all 13 essential core conserved T6SS components... were recently designated TssA to TssM in an attempt to unify the nomenclature.
machinery_genes.note: Part of the collectively-named TssA-TssM core set of the Db10 cluster. Role from apparatus brief. Moderate-confidence (collective naming).

machinery_genes.quote: The cluster contains all 13 essential core conserved T6SS components... were recently designated TssA to TssM in an attempt to unify the nomenclature.
machinery_genes.note: Part of the collectively-named TssA-TssM core set. Role from apparatus brief. Moderate-confidence (collective naming).

machinery_genes.quote: we constructed in-frame deletion mutants in genes encoding... ClpV (SMA2274)
machinery_genes.note: ClpV (TssH) explicitly named and mutated (SMA2274) in the Db10 T6SS. Essential machinery (sheath recycling); counted per brief. Naming also corroborated in Fritsch 2013: 'the AAA+ ATPase ClpV (TssH)'.

machinery_genes.quote: It contains two VgrG homologues, which do not possess recognizable evolved C-terminal effector domains, and one Hcp homologue.
machinery_genes.note: Two VgrG (TssI) homologues encoded in the Db10 cluster; counted as machinery (secreted spike) per brief. The cluster's VgrGs lack evolved C-terminal effector domains.

machinery_genes.quote: the outer membrane lipoprotein TssJ, which stimulates formation of the MC through the recruitment of TssL and TssM
machinery_genes.note: TssJ named as a core outer-membrane lipoprotein of the S. marcescens Db10 T6SS membrane complex (Reglinski 2023); tssJ = SMDB11_2252 in this genome (= Lip / SMA2252 in Murdoch 2011, where it was mutated as 'Lip (SMA2252)').

machinery_genes.quote: The cluster contains all 13 essential core conserved T6SS components... were recently designated TssA to TssM in an attempt to unify the nomenclature.
machinery_genes.note: Part of the collectively-named TssA-TssM core set of the Db10 cluster. Role from apparatus brief. Moderate-confidence (collective naming).

machinery_genes.quote: the outer membrane lipoprotein TssJ, which stimulates formation of the MC through the recruitment of TssL and TssM
machinery_genes.note: TssL named as a membrane complex protein of the S. marcescens Db10 T6SS (Reglinski 2023); the paper also reports 'TagV did not interact with the T6SS membrane complex proteins TssL or TssM'. Corresponds to Murdoch's IcmH-related membrane complex region.

machinery_genes.quote: The MC comprises two concentric rings of TssM pillars which span the periplasmic space and are linked to TssJ
machinery_genes.note: TssM (IcmF-like) named as the central membrane complex protein of the S. marcescens Db10 T6SS (Reglinski 2023). Murdoch 2011 mutated the adjacent IcmH (SMA2254) of this membrane-complex region.

machinery_genes.quote: The basic T6SS machinery comprises fourteen well-characterised core components (denoted TssA-M and PAAR)... The puncturing device comprises a tube of stacked hexameric Hcp rings topped with a VgrG/PAAR spike.
machinery_genes.note: PAAR named as one of the 14 core machinery components of the S. marcescens Db10 T6SS (Reglinski 2023). Counted as machinery (secreted tip) per brief.

machinery_genes.role: accessory FHA-domain regulatory component (PpkA-phosphorylated; required for firing)
machinery_genes.quote: Deletion of the Fha homolog SMA2267 in S. marcescens also abolished Hcp secretion, demonstrating an essential role for Fha in this T6SS
machinery_genes.note: Fha (TagH) named as an essential structural/regulatory component of the Db10 T6SS (Fritsch 2013); locus SMA2267. PpkA-dependent threonine phosphorylation of Fha is required for activation in this organism.

machinery_genes.role: accessory Ser/Thr kinase (phosphorylates Fha to activate T6SS)
machinery_genes.quote: the kinase activity of PpkA is essential for T6SS activity in S. marcescens
machinery_genes.note: PpkA (TagE) named as the activating kinase of the Db10 T6SS (Fritsch 2013); locus SMA2275. Accessory post-translational regulator.

machinery_genes.quote: conserved PpkA-dependent threonine phosphorylation of the T6SS structural component Fha is required for T6SS activation in S. marcescens and that the phosphatase PppA can reverse this modification
machinery_genes.note: PppA (TagG) named as the phosphatase counteracting PpkA in the Db10 T6SS (Fritsch 2013); locus SMA2268. Accessory post-translational regulator.

machinery_genes.quote: we have identified a novel accessory component required for optimal T6SS activity in the opportunistic pathogen Serratia marcescens, which we name TagV. Deletion of tagV, which encodes an outer membrane lipoprotein, caused a reduction in the T6SS-dependent antibacterial activity of S. marcescens Db10.
machinery_genes.note: TagV named and characterized as a S. marcescens Db10 T6SS accessory outer-membrane lipoprotein (Reglinski 2023); tagV = SMDB11_2251, adjacent to tssJ (SMDB11_2252). Supports a second, TssJ-independent firing mechanism.

notes: Serratia marcescens Db10 carries a single, well-characterized antibacterial T6SS in one chromosomal cluster (SMA2243-SMA2281 in the Murdoch 2011 annotation; SMDB11_ locus tags in the NZ_HG326223.1 / Db11 assembly). The founding paper (Murdoch et al 2011, J Bacteriol) states the cluster 'contains all 13 essential core conserved T6SS components' designated TssA to TssM, secretes Hcp (SMA2263), encodes two VgrG and one cluster Hcp homologue, and was dissected by deletion of ClpV (SMA2274), Lip/TssJ (SMA2252), IcmH (SMA2254) and TssE (SMA2271). Core membrane-complex naming (TssJ, TssL, TssM) and PAAR are taken from Reglinski et al 2023 (the TagV paper), which states the S. marcescens machinery 'comprises fourteen well-characterised core components (denoted TssA-M and PAAR)'. Accessory regulation (Fha/TagH = SMA2267, PpkA/TagE = SMA2275, PppA/TagG = SMA2268) is from Fritsch et al 2013. All named for THIS organism/strain; no cross-strain transfer needed. Per the brief, Hcp (TssD), VgrG (TssI) and PAAR ARE counted as machinery (secreted tube/spike/tip). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: delivered toxin effectors and immunity proteins (e.g. the Ssp/Rhs antibacterial effectors and EagR1 accessory of Alcoforado Diniz 2015, and the four Fritsch 2013 effectors), DsbA (host-housekeeping periplasmic oxidase, not a T6SS-specific structural gene; Mariano 2018), and the effector context loci (SMDB11_2270, 2278-2280, 2342-2343, 2369, 4259). LOW-CONFIDENCE CALLS: TssA, TssB, TssC, TssF, TssG, TssK are supported only by the collective 'all 13... designated TssA to TssM' naming quote from Murdoch 2011 (no individual per-gene functional quote was located); they are included because the founding paper explicitly states all 13 TssA-TssM components are present in this single cluster, and roles are assigned from the apparatus brief vocabulary. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PMC full texts / abstracts; all four DOIs and PMIDs were confirmed via PubMed get_article_metadata. Source: PubMed.
